title: Rich Pillowy French Pilaf
description: A flavorful French pilaf dish with a rich, pillowy texture, perfect for a crowd-pleasing lunch or dinner. This pilaf recipe is a delicious Ayurvedic-rajasic-friendly option, cooked to perfection in a wok.
instructions: In a wok, heat the duck fat over medium heat. Add the butter and let melt.
Add the mustard and cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute.
Add the thyme, bay leaves, and garlic; cook for an additional 1-2 minutes.
Add the rice and stir to coat with the fat and herb mixture.
Add the chicken broth and white wine; bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15 minutes.
Remove the lid and fluff the pilaf with a fork.
Add the salt and black pepper, and serve hot.
Let the pilaf rest for 5 minutes before serving to allow the flavors to meld together.
This step is crucial for achieving the rich, pillowy texture of the pilaf.
